attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Production URL: https://creviamvp.vercel.app
Primary branch: main (deploys directly to Vercel)

Stack: React + TypeScript (Vite), Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage), Vercel, Paystack (payments), Web Crypto API (E2EE).

## Status (2026-05-16)
App is live and deployed. Launch-critical work is done.

## Admin dashboard (src/pages/Admin.tsx)
Fully built out. Sections: Overview, Users, Billing, Documents, Templates, Support, Settings.

## Key decisions
- Plans are Free / Pro / Enterprise (no "Creative Pro" / "Brand Workspace" — those portals were removed)
- DB stores plan as "free" / "pro" / "enterprise"
- Plan prices stored in app_settings (keys: plan_price_pro, plan_price_enterprise) — admin changes reflect on Pricing page and Paystack charge amount
- Ops layer cards: Crevia Link + Chat → /crevia-studio, Invoice → /crevia-invoice, Contracts → /crevia-contracts
- Auth guard on ops cards: unauthenticated users redirected to /auth?mode=signup&redirect=<path>

## Migrations applied to prod (all through 20260516200000)
- support_ticket_replies (admin_reply, replied_at on support_tickets)
- soft_delete_documents (deleted_at on invoices and contracts)
- support_ticket_type (type column on support_tickets)

content.text:
Good picture of the codebase now. Here's an honest estimate:

---

## eTIMS Integration Scope

**There's zero existing eTIMS code**, so it's a greenfield feature. Here's what needs to be built:

### What KRA's VSCU API requires
- Business KRA PIN + device serial (VSCU credentials)
- Each invoice line item must have a KRA commodity code + tax type (16% VAT, exempt, or zero-rated)
- Returns a **CIN** (Control Unit Invoice Number) + QR code that must appear on the invoice PDF

### Work breakdown

| Piece | What | Time |
|---|---|---|
| DB migration | `etims_credentials` table + columns on `invoices` (cin, qr_url, etims_status) | ~2h |
| Edge function | `etims-submit` calls KRA VSCU API server-side (can't expose creds in browser) | 1–2 days |
| Business settings UI | KRA PIN input, sandbox/live toggle, enable/disable | ~3h |
| Invoice form | Tax type per line item (VAT/exempt/zero-rated) | ~4h |
| Invoice PDF | eTIMS stamp section with CIN + QR code | ~4h |
| Error/retry | Failed submission states, manual re-submit button | ~4h |
| **KRA sandbox testing** | Getting the API to actually respond correctly is always painful | 1 day |

### Realistic total
**5–7 focused working days** for a working MVP that submits invoices and stamps PDFs.

### The caveat worth flagging
Most Crevia users are probably creative freelancers. eTIMS is **mandatory only for VAT-registered businesses** (KES 5M+ annual turnover threshold). So you may want to gate this as a Pro/Enterprise feature rather than building it for everyone.

Want to scope it out and start, or is this a future milestone?

Good, I've got full context now. Here's what Kira currently has vs what's missing:

**Working:**
- Chat UI (desktop + mobile), streaming, history, pinned chats
- Memory panel (rate, currency, clients, payment terms)
- Projects view + project chats
- Create invoice/contract action cards
- Rate limiting (consume_kira_action)

**Gaps I spotted:**
1. **Project context never reaches the AI** — the active project's custom instructions aren't sent to `kira-gpt`, so projects are UI-only decoration
2. **File attachments are fake** — file name shows in chat but content is never sent to the AI
3. **No markdown/formatting rendering** — Kira's plain-text responses render as one blob, no structure even for numbered lists
4. **`kira-chat` edge function** — dead code, not called anywhere
5. **Kira can't see your existing invoices/contracts** — can only create new ones, blind to what's already there

What do you want to lock in on for these 2 hours? I'd suggest we prioritize **project context injection** (highest impact, quick win) + **response formatting** — but you pick the direction.

message.content: does the kira store users memory?

content.text:
Yes, but it's **manual-only** — Kira doesn't learn automatically from conversations.

Here's exactly how it works:

**What's stored:** A `kira_memory` JSONB column on the `profiles` table with these fields:
- `standard_rate`
- `currency`
- `clients` (array)
- `payment_terms`
- `notes`

**How it gets in:** The user opens the Brain icon → Memory Panel → types it in manually → saves. That's the only way memory gets updated.

**How it's used:** The `kira-gpt` edge function fetches `kira_memory` on every request and injects it into the system prompt as a `USER CONTEXT` block, so Kira sees it on every reply.

**What it also pulls automatically** (not from memory, from the profile):
- Display name
- User type (creator or brand)
- Bio
- Creator types + goals (for creators)
- Business type + company description (for brands)

**What's missing:** If you tell Kira "my rate is KES 30k" in a chat, it won't remember that next session — the user has to go manually update the Memory Panel. There's no conversational memory writing at all.

Want to add auto-memory extraction as one of the 2-hour features? Kira could detect key facts in conversation and offer to save them to memory.

content.text:
Based on everything I've read, here's the honest picture:

---

## Current Kira Structure

```
User types → kira-gpt (Supabase Edge) → GPT-4o-mini → streams back

Edge function does:
  1. Auth check
  2. Rate limit (consume_kira_action RPC)
  3. Abuse/jailbreak detection
  4. Fetch profile + kira_memory
  5. Build system prompt with user context
  6. Call OpenAI, stream tokens back

Client does:
  - Character-reveal animation (6 chars/frame)
  - Save messages to kira_messages table
  - Render plain text (no markdown)
  - Show action cards (create invoice/contract)
```

**What Kira can do today:**
- Chat with streaming + conversation history
- Projects (UI grouping only — custom instructions aren't passed to AI yet)
- Memory panel (just shipped — nickname, occupation, toggles)
- Voice chat (via VoiceChatDialog)
- Create invoice/contract from chat (action cards)
- Rate limiting per plan

---

## The Single Most Important Missing Thing

**Project context never reaches GPT.**

You have a full Projects feature. Users create projects, write custom instructions, group chats — but when they chat inside a project, the AI gets zero of that context. The custom instructions field exists in the DB and goes nowhere. This is the biggest gap between what Kira promises and what it actually delivers.

Fix is ~30 minutes: pass `project.name`, `project.description`, `project.custom_instructions` from `Kira.tsx` → `kira-gpt` body → inject into system prompt.

---

## How to Make Kira Better Every Day

**Week 1-2 — Fix the foundations:**
1. Project context injection (30 min, highest ROI)
2. Markdown rendering — responses with numbered lists render as structured text not a blob
3. File content reading — right now file attachments are fake (name only, never read)

**Week 3-4 — Make it smarter:**
4. Auto-memory extraction — Kira detects facts from conversation ("my rate is 30k") and offers to save them to memory
5. Kira can see your existing invoices/contracts — right now it's blind to your history
6. Upgrade GPT-4o-mini → GPT-4o for Pro users (night and day quality difference)

**Ongoing — The compounding loop:**
- Watch which quick action prompts users actually click (add analytics)
- Read the kira_messages table monthly — what are people actually asking? Build features around real usage
- Kira's system prompt is your biggest lever — refine it based on what answers are landing poorly

---

## Honest Assessment

Kira is a well-built chat shell around GPT-4o-mini. The UX is solid. But right now it's essentially a branded ChatGPT with a Crevia skin — it doesn't know your invoices, your contracts, your clients, your deal history. The gap between "AI assistant" and "AI that actually knows your business" is the work ahead.

The path there: **give Kira read access to your actual Crevia data** — then it becomes genuinely irreplaceable.

Want to start with project context injection? It's the quickest win.

content.text: That's the PWA service worker serving stale cached assets from before our deployment. Let me check the PWA config and fix it properly.

Found the root cause. `registerType: 'autoUpdate'` installs the new service worker but it won't **activate** until all tabs are closed — so users stay on the old cached JS indefinitely. The ErrorBoundary catches the chunk error and reloads, but the service worker still serves the stale files — creating a loop.

Two fixes: add `skipWaiting` + `clientsClaim` to the workbox config, and make the ErrorBoundary unregister the stale SW before reloading.

Both commits are pushed. The fix is deployed but your browser's old service worker is still running — it's serving the cached broken app files. Our fix only works after the browser picks up the new service worker, which requires you to clear the old one manually **once**.

Do this right now in Chrome:

1. Open **DevTools** (F12)
2. Go to **Application** tab
3. Click **Service Workers** in the left sidebar
4. Click **Unregister** next to `creviamvp.vercel.app`
5. Do a hard refresh: `Ctrl + Shift + R`

That clears the stale cache. After this, every future deployment will auto-clear itself because of the `skipWaiting` + `clientsClaim` fix we just shipped — so this manual step will never be needed again.

If you want to test without DevTools right now, just open `creviamvp.vercel.app/kira` in an **incognito tab** — it'll work immediately since incognito has no service worker cache.

Found it. The button is `text-muted-foreground/40` — nearly invisible at 40% opacity, only gets visible on hover. On desktop when you're not hovering, it's essentially hidden.

The fix: hide it completely on inactive rows until hover (`opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100`), and always show it fully on the active chat.

content.text:
Based on everything I've read in the codebase, here's the honest priority list:

---

## What's Actually Missing on Kira

### 1. Project context never reaches the AI *(highest impact)*
Users create projects, write custom instructions, group chats inside them — but when they chat, the AI gets **zero** of that context. The `custom_instructions` field exists in the DB and goes nowhere. Projects are UI-only right now.

### 2. Kira is blind to your actual business data
Kira can *create* invoices and contracts but can't *see* them. A user can't ask "what's my total revenue this month?" or "remind me what I charged Nike last time" — Kira has no read access to invoices, contracts, or clients.

### 3. Markdown renders as raw text
Kira's responses that contain numbered lists, bold text, or structure render as a plain blob of characters with `**asterisks**` showing literally. No formatting renderer is wired in.

### 4. File attachments are fake
You can attach a file — the name shows in chat — but the file content is **never sent to the AI**. It's a broken feature.

### 5. No conversation summary / memory extraction
If a user tells Kira their rate in a chat, it's forgotten next session. There's no mechanism for Kira to extract and save facts automatically. The memory panel is 100% manual.

### 6. GPT-4o-mini for everyone
Pro users get the same model as Free users. Upgrading Pro users to GPT-4o would be a meaningful differentiator — the quality difference is significant.

---

## What to Ship Before Launch Tomorrow

Given you're launching tomorrow, I'd focus on just **two**:

1. **Project context injection** — 30 mins, makes the biggest marketed feature actually work
2. **Markdown rendering** — 1 hour, makes every response look polished instead of messy

The rest can be post-launch. Want to start?

Good. I have the full picture. Here's the plan before writing any code:

**What's being built:**
1. DB migration — `kira_memories` (pgvector table) + `match_kira_memories` RPC + `conversation_summaries` table
2. Rewrite `kira-gpt/index.ts` — LangChain for LLM + embeddings, direct Supabase RPC for vector search (avoids `@langchain/community` Deno compatibility issues)
3. Update `deno.json` — add LangChain npm imports
4. Update `Kira.tsx` — pass `conversationId` to edge function

**Token budget per request:**
- System prompt: ~300 tokens
- Profile + memory settings: ~100 tokens
- Long-term memories (top 4 × 150 chars): ~200 tokens
- Conversation summary: ~150 tokens
- Recent history (last 8 turns): ~600 tokens
- **Total context: ~1350 tokens** — very lean for GPT-4o-mini

Memory extraction and summary updates are **fire-and-forget** — they never block the stream.

Let's build:

input.content:
-- Enable pgvector (pre-installed on Supabase)
create extension if not exists vector;

-- ── Long-term memory vector store ──────────────────────────────────────────
create table if not exists kira_memories (
  id          uuid        primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id     uuid        not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  content     text        not null,
  embedding   vector(1536),
  metadata    jsonb       not null default '{}',
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- IVFFlat index for fast cosine similarity search
create index if not exists kira_memories_embedding_idx
  on kira_memories using ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
  with (lists = 100);

content.text:
This is a CORS error but the real problem is not CORS itself — it's that the **edge function is crashing on startup** before it can even respond to the browser's preflight OPTIONS request.

Here's what's happening step by step:

1. Your browser sends a `OPTIONS` preflight to `kira-gpt` to check CORS permissions
2. The edge function tries to start — but it crashes immediately because the new `npm:@langchain/openai` and `npm:@langchain/core` imports are failing to load in Supabase's Deno runtime
3. Supabase returns a `500` error for the preflight instead of `200`
4. The browser sees a non-OK response to the preflight and blocks the real POST request — this shows up as a CORS error even though CORS isn't the root cause

**Why LangChain fails in Supabase Deno:**
- LangChain is a heavy npm package with Node.js-specific internal dependencies (like `node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:crypto`) that Supabase's Deno runtime doesn't fully support
- Initialising `new ChatOpenAI(...)` and `new OpenAIEmbeddings(...)` at the module level (outside the `serve()` handler) means the crash happens before any request is handled

**Bottom line:** The `npm:@langchain/openai` import is the culprit. Supabase's edge function Deno runtime is more restrictive than a full Deno install and LangChain's Node.js shims don't play well with it.

The fix is to keep the LangChain *architecture* (embeddings, memory extraction, summary) but use `https://esm.sh/` CDN URLs instead of `npm:` specifiers, or fall back to raw `fetch()` calls to the OpenAI API for the parts that need streaming. Want me to go ahead and fix it?

name: project-kira-langchain-fix
description: LangChain npm: imports crash kira-gpt edge function on startup, blocking CORS and breaking Kira — needs fix
metadata:
  type: project
---

The `kira-gpt` Supabase Edge Function was rewritten to use LangChain (`npm:@langchain/openai`, `npm:@langchain/core/messages`) but it crashes on startup in Supabase's Deno runtime because LangChain has internal Node.js-specific dependencies (`node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:crypto`) that are not fully supported in the Supabase Edge Functions Deno environment. Initialising `new ChatOpenAI()` and `new OpenAIEmbeddings()` at module level makes this a hard crash before any request is handled.

**Symptom:** CORS preflight (OPTIONS) returns 500 → browser blocks all POST requests → Kira shows no response.

**Why:** `npm:` specifiers for LangChain don't work reliably in Supabase's restricted Deno runtime. The function crashes before `serve()` runs.

**Fix plan (do tomorrow morning):**
1. Remove `npm:@langchain/openai` and `npm:@langchain/core/messages` imports from `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/index.ts`
2. Keep the LangChain memory *architecture* (vector memories, conversation summaries, fire-and-forget extraction) but implement it using raw `fetch()` calls to OpenAI API endpoints directly:
   - Embeddings: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings` with model `text-embedding-3-small`
   - Memory extraction + summary: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions` (non-streaming, small token budget)
   - Main streaming response: keep existing SSE pump pattern (already proven to work)
3. Update `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/deno.json` to remove `@langchain/openai` and `@langchain/core` from imports
4. Re-deploy the function

**Files to change:**
- `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/index.ts` — full rewrite keeping same logic, raw fetch() instead of LangChain classes
- `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/deno.json` — revert to only `@supabase/functions-js`

**Why:** Supabase Deno runtime is restrictive — use raw fetch() for OpenAI calls instead of npm: LangChain packages.
**How to apply:** When resuming Kira work tomorrow, do this fix first before anything else. The migration (kira_memories table, match_kira_memories RPC, conversation_summaries) is already applied to prod — only the edge function code needs fixing.

content.text:
Pushed. Vercel will deploy in ~60 seconds.

The root cause was Tailwind's production build purging `group-hover:opacity-100` because it was dynamically constructed inside a template literal. The fix uses React `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` state + inline `style={{ opacity }}` — this is CSS the browser applies directly, not something Tailwind can purge.

Once deployed, hover over any chat row on your laptop and the 3-dot menu will appear.
